# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that copies user-uploaded files from a mounted volume into the container for processing. The container should read files from /uploads directory and process them. Use COPY and WORKDIR to set up the file structure. This is for our file processing pipeline.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:3.11-slim

# Create a non-root user for security
RUN groupadd --gid 1001 appgroup && \
    useradd --uid 1001 --gid appgroup --shell /bin/false --create-home appuser

# Set working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Create the uploads directory with appropriate permissions
RUN mkdir -p /uploads && \
    chown appuser:appgroup /uploads && \
    chmod 750 /uploads

# Create processing output directory
RUN mkdir -p /app/output && \
    chown appuser:appgroup /app/output && \
    chmod 750 /app/output

# Copy application code (processing scripts)
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup process.py /app/process.py

# Install any needed dependencies
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup requirements.txt /app/requirements.txt
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip && \
    pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Drop all unnecessary capabilities
# Run container as read-only filesystem where possible
# The /uploads directory will be mounted as a volume at runtime

# Set restrictive file permissions on application code
RUN chmod 500 /app/process.py && \
    chmod 400 /app/requirements.txt

# Do NOT use COPY for user-uploaded files — they come via volume mount at runtime
# This prevents baking untrusted content into the image layer
VOLUME ["/uploads"]

# Health check to ensure the container is running properly
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD ["python", "-c", "import os; assert os.path.isdir('/uploads')"]

# Switch to non-root user before running
USER appuser

# Prevent privilege escalation
# Note: Use --security-opt=no-new-privileges:true at runtime

# Set environment variables for secure operation
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    UPLOAD_DIR=/uploads \
    OUTPUT_DIR=/app/output

# Use exec form to avoid shell injection
ENTRYPOINT ["python", "/app/process.py"]

# Default arguments (can be overridden)
CMD ["--input-dir", "/uploads", "--output-dir", "/app/output"]
